message:
copy(section-title): rename "What You Already Know" to "What You May Already Know"

Softens the PAR2/Reed-Solomon bridge section heading from a declarative
"What You Already Know" to the more inclusive "What You May Already Know."

The original phrasing assumed familiarity with PAR2 files and RAID 5,
which risks alienating readers who haven't encountered those tools.
The hedged version ("May") preserves the rhetorical bridge—connecting
erasure coding to something concrete—while acknowledging that not
every reader arrives with that specific background.

Single-word change on line 168: "Already" → "May Already".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -165,7 +165,7 @@

The Impossible Promise

-

What You Already Know

+

What You May Already Know

If you've ever used PAR2 files to repair a corrupted download, or relied on RAID 5 to survive a dead drive, you've already seen the seed of this idea. PAR2 uses Reed-Solomon codes to generate repair blocks from your original data. Lose a few files? Throw in some .par2 recovery blocks, and the repair tool reconstructs the missing pieces. Any recovery block helps—you don't need specific ones.
